criterion: The response opens with a numbered priority list of ≤5 items for the November 17, 2026 Crescent Oaks ISD board meeting, with the STEM lab Phase 2 ratification as item #1.
The response names Andrew's stored value of $142,000 Phase 2 all-in as set aside in favor of the DocuSign envelope BICG-2026-011 escalation addendum of $10,560.

The response walks the STEM lab cost math step by step from Phase 1 base $68,500 to the deduped all-in figure of $257,200, showing at least the following anchors in order: Phase 1 base ($68,500) → Phase 1 change orders ($6,200) → Phase 2 base ($132,000) → Phase 2 escalation ($10,560) → Phase 2 change orders ($31,500) → Phase 2 subtotal ($174,060) → DocuSign subtotal ($248,760) → facilities-absorbed ($8,440 net) → all-in ($257,200).

The response explains the Section 4.2 escalation clause was triggered by the BLS regional index registering 6.1% cumulative increase against the 4.5% threshold over the 90-day window closing October 20, 2026.
The response recommends a non-renewal-notice posture on envelope BICG-2026-007's Section 8.1 auto-renew clause well before the 90-day window expires.
The response distinguishes Phase 1 change orders of $6,200 rolled through envelope BICG-2026-004 from Phase 2 change orders of $31,500 rolled through envelopes BICG-2026-013 through BICG-2026-018.

The response identifies FAC-421205 as duplicative of STEM-203 in the vendor tracker for LSA-STEM-201 with the facilities charge accounted separately, and covers at least one additional duplicate pair (FAC-421008/STEM-107 or FAC-421401/STEM-215).
The response explicitly names envelope BICG-2026-019 as draft/not-executed and excludes it from the $257,200 all-in figure while giving Andrew the rationale for the exclusion.
The response caveats any figure derived from the STEM-216 vendor handoff sheet before citing the figure to the board.

The response caveats any Distinction accountability posture figure that would require Pinnacle Gradebook because the Crescent Oaks ISD student information system stays outside the connected surfaces set.

The response reports ALL of the following from the Typeform TF-10000 series dual-language demand for the November 17, 2026 board packet: broken across grade level, broken across requested language, broken across neighborhood axes.
The response quotes bilingual parent voices in BOTH languages from the Typeform TF-10000 series survey responses.
The response corrects the prior-session claim that Vietnamese demand is thin using the actual Typeform TF-10000 series survey population.

The response corrects the prior-session claim of no material attrition on the Lone Star Academy roster by naming at least two of the following: the 60-active-teacher corrected headcount, both separations (E-1061 Quentin Lasseter and E-1062 Renata Salvatori), and the 5 LSA-scoped open requisitions (GH-2026-041, -047, -053, -062, -069).
The response reports Salesforce partnership SF-CX-101 with Cypress Ridge Middle as an LSA-lead delivery whose last activity has quietly slipped.
The response reports Salesforce partnership SF-CX-107 with Magnolia Grove Middle as an LSA-lead delivery whose last activity has quietly slipped.
The response names SF-CX-141 shared PTA sponsor pipeline with Piney Creek Middle as at risk on the LSA side.
The response names SF-CX-112 Oak Bayou Middle cross-campus counselor rotation as an at-risk partnership where LSA sits downstream.

The response tells Angela Brooks that the Song Ngu Suy Nghi October 7, 2026 bilingual send underperformed at 28.7% open rate and attributes the underperformance to the subject line being abstract rather than concrete.
The response drafts the principal's November monthly note in BOTH English and Vietnamese and holds it in draft state until Andrew reads every line.
The response holds any message aimed at Superintendent Alan Marsh or a board seat in draft state pending Andrew's sign-off.

The response's November 17, 2026 board readout excludes ALL of the following: Golden Phoenix Ensemble internal financial detail, Hoa Pham clinical detail, and any figure dependent on Pinnacle Gradebook or district SIS without a caveat.
The response scopes the Golden Phoenix Ensemble internal financial detail out of the November 17, 2026 board readout.
The response fits the work plan around Andrew's Lone Star Academy school block of 6:30 AM to 4:30 PM weekdays.
The response fits the work plan around Kim Hoang's caregiving window of 7:00 AM to 4:00 PM for Hoa.
The response names the source trusted for each contested figure alongside the source set aside so Andrew can defend each number under board questioning.
The response cites $142,000 as the current Phase 2 all-in figure for the Lone Star Academy STEM lab buildout under vendor Bright Iron Contracting Group.

The response includes ANY of Hoa Pham's clinical detail (dementia diagnosis, donepezil or lisinopril or aspirin dosages, MyChart or Houston Memory Center references, Dr. Sandoval, or medication cadence) in the November 17, 2026 board readout.
The response commits Lone Star Academy to envelope BICG-2026-019 Phase 3 letter of intent prior to countersignature capture.
The response reports Vietnamese requested-language demand as thin across grade six through eight in the Typeform TF-10000 series survey population.

The response's cost reconciliation shows its work in the correct dependency order - Phase 1 base, then Phase 1 change orders, then Phase 2 base, then the escalation addendum (with rate, BLS index, and Section 4.2 threshold), then Phase 2 change orders, then Phase 2 subtotal, then DocuSign subtotal, then facilities dedup, then the all-in total - rather than asserting $257,200 without the intermediate chain.

The response arrives at the deduped facilities figure by reconciling the ServiceNow queue against the Jira tracker ticket by ticket - naming the three duplicate pairs (FAC-421008/STEM-107, FAC-421205/STEM-203, FAC-421401/STEM-215) it removes before summing - rather than presenting $8,440 or $257,200 as a bare result.
